confirmed.title: AiSelectInput suggestion 'Accept' button: visible text hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: After an AI classification suggestion, the accept button renders the bare JSX text 'Accept' (line 284) with no t() wrapping and no aria-label. Sibling AI inputs (AiImageInput, AiExtractInput) route their apply button through t('arqel.ai.apply_all', ...). The provenance badge two lines below ('Suggested by AI'/'Used fallback') was already keyed in round-fix, but these accept/dismiss buttons were missed. Visible (not aria) untranslated string.
confirmed.why_real: Confirmed at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/ai/src/AiSelectInput.tsx:284. The "Accept" button renders bare JSX text 'Accept' (line 284), and the sibling "Pick another" button at line 293 is identically hardcoded — both with no t() wrapping and no aria-label. These are visible, user-facing strings rendered after an AI classification suggestion (inside the suggestion !== null block). The component already imports useArqelTranslations and has `t` in scope (line 126), and uses it everywhere else (button label line 130, status line 255, provenance badge lines 274-275), so this is a genuine miss, not an architectural limitation. Not vendored: the file is in packages-js/ai/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Not a duplicate: the ledger contains four AiSelectInput entries (R4 error strings @183, R5 config error @165, R9 provenance badge @271, R10 NO_CONTEXT_TOOLTIP @224) but NONE cover the Accept/Pick-another suggestion-action buttons. The R9 fix keyed the provenance badge two lines above but missed the adjacent action buttons, exactly as the finding states. The two unrelated ledger "accept/dismiss" hits (flash toast Dismiss @215, Accept-Language parser @791) are different files/concerns.

confirmed.why_real: Verified packages-js/ai/src/AiSelectInput.tsx line 293: the "Pick another" button renders bare JSX text with no t() call and no aria-label; its sibling "Accept" at line 284 has the same defect. Both are visible, user-facing English strings in a first-party (non-vendored, not shadcn) AI field component. confirmed.title: MarkdownInput 'Link' toolbar button: visible glyph text hardcoded English while aria-label is translated
confirmed.detail: The link toolbar button has a translated aria-label (t('arqel.fields_advanced.markdown_link','Link') at line ~464) but its visible button-face content is the raw word 'Link' (line 470). Inconsistent with sibling glyph buttons that use language-neutral symbols (B, I, H, <>). aria-vs-visible mismatch: the visible label stays English in every locale. Round-fix keyed the aria-labels (ledger line 430) and the Preview/fullscreen visible text but missed Link/List visible glyphs.
confirmed.why_real:
Confirmed at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/fields-advanced/src/markdown/MarkdownInput.tsx. The Link button (lines 463-471) and List button (472-480) each have a translated aria-label via t() (markdown_link line 468; markdown_list line 477) but their visible button-face content is the raw English word 'Link' (line 470) and 'List' (line 479). Unlike sibling glyph buttons B/I/H/<> (lines 434,443,452,461) which use language-neutral symbols, Link/List render actual translatable words. This is a genuine aria-vs-visible mismatch: the visible label stays English in every locale while the accessible name is localized. User-facing toolbar buttons.

Not a duplicate of R1-R11. The ledger's MarkdownInput entries are: line 430/587 (round 4) = aria-labels keyed (which is precisely why the aria-labels are now translated but the visible text was not); line 491 (round 10) = Preview *visible* text; line 501 (round 10) = Exit/Full fullscreen *visible* text; line 498 (round 10) = fullscreen aria-label. The Link (470) and List (479) *visible* text entries are absent from the ledger — round 10 swept the Preview and fullscreen visible glyphs but missed Link/List. Not in packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/* (it is fields-advanced, not vendored shadcn).

confirmed.why_real:

Not a duplicate. The ledger's MarkdownInput visible-text entries (Round 10) cover ONLY the Preview button visible text (line 491) and the fullscreen Exit/Full toggle visible text (line 501). Round 4 (line 430) addressed only the aria-labels ("every aria-label hardcoded English"), which the current code shows are now translated. The visible "List" (and sibling "Link", line 470) toolbar button faces are NOT in the ledger under any round — a grep for List/Link/visible-face entries returns no match for these buttons. Distinct, novel location not previously fixed.

Note: the formatting buttons B/I/H/<> are language-neutral glyphs, but 'Link' and 'List' are full English words used as visible labels, so the defect is genuine and locale-affecting.

confirmed.title: MarkdownInput popup 'Close' button: visible text hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: The fullscreen/popup preview dialog's dismiss button renders bare JSX text 'Close' (line 587) with neither t() nor an aria-label. Fully untranslated and unlabeled. Visible untranslated string.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/fields-advanced/src/markdown/MarkdownInput.tsx:587. The popup preview <dialog>'s dismiss <button> (lines 582-588) renders bare JSX text 'Close' with no t() and no aria-label. The t() translator (useArqelTranslations, line 308) is in scope and used by every sibling control in this same component, including the Preview button at lines 488/490 in popup mode, so this is a genuine inconsistency, not a no-translator file. The popup path is reachable: showPopup = showPreview && mode === 'popup' (line 402); clicking the Preview button sets isPopupOpen, rendering the dialog with this untranslated Close button. User-facing visible string. Not vendored: this is the framework's own MarkdownInput, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Ledger check: file has entries at round 4 line 430 (editor-controls aria-label sweep) and round 10 lines 491/498/501 (Preview visible text, fullscreen toggle aria-label, fullscreen visible text 'Exit'/'Full') — none of these cover the popup dialog Close button at line 587. Novel, not a duplicate.

confirmed.title: RichTextInput 'Link' toolbar glyph: visible button face hardcoded English
confirmed.detail: The link toolbar entry's render() returns the literal word 'Link' as the button's visible face (line 201, render: () => <>Link</>). The button's aria-label IS translated at the render site (line 415 uses spec.labelKey). Every sibling entry renders a language-neutral glyph (H1, S, •, 1., '<>') — only Link uses an English word, so it stays English in all locales. Visible-vs-aria inconsistency.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified in /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages-js/fields-advanced/src/rich-text/RichTextInput.tsx. Line 201: `link: { id: 'link', labelKey: 'richtext_link', labelFallback: 'Link', render: () => <>Link</> }`. The render() output is the visible button content (line 425: `{spec.render()}`). Every sibling BUTTON_SPECS entry renders a language-neutral glyph (B, I, U, S, H1/H2/H3, •, 1., ", <>, Img); only `link` returns the English word "Link". The aria-label/title ARE localized via t() at line 407/415, but the visible glyph is not, so the button face reads "Link" in all locales — a real visible-vs-aria i18n inconsistency, user-facing in the rich-text toolbar.

Not vendored: file is under fields-advanced/src/rich-text/, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

Not a ledger duplicate: existing R7/R9 entries for this file cover line 384 (max-length error), 393 (char counter), 162 (BUTTON_SPECS aria-label/title localization), 376 (image-disabled title), 221 (editor placeholder). The R9 line-162 entry addresses the accessible-name localization (which the code now does via t() at line 407). None of the entries address the visible render() glyph content of the link button at line 201. Novel.

confirmed.title: PluginAutoChecker emits 8 hardcoded English check messages serialized to the plugin-submission UI
confirmed.detail: check() runs on every POST plugin submission via PluginSubmissionController and its {name,status,message} report is persisted and shown to the submitting publisher. Round 11 localized ONLY the screenshots_count OK message (line 133, trans_choice). The other 8 message strings are still raw English literals: line 59 'composer_package must match vendor/package (lowercase alnum + hyphens).'; line 66 'Composer package follows vendor/package convention.'; line 81 'github_url must point to github.com.'; line 88 'GitHub URL points to github.com.'; line 103 'Description is short; consider 50+ characters for better discoverability.'; line 110 'Description length is adequate.'; line 126 'No screenshots provided; at least one is recommended.'; line 151 'Another plugin already uses this name.'; line 158 'Plugin name is unique.'. All reach the end user verbatim, never routed through __()/trans(). Framework-hardcoded.
confirmed.why_real:
Verified against the source file and the R1-R11 ledger.

REAL: PluginAutoChecker.php contains 8 raw hardcoded English message literals never routed through __()/trans()/trans_choice(): line 59 ('composer_package must match vendor/package...'), 66 ('Composer package follows vendor/package convention.'), 81 ('github_url must point to github.com.'), 88 ('GitHub URL points to github.com.'), 103 ('Description is short; consider 50+ characters...'), 110 ('Description length is adequate.'), 126 ('No screenshots provided; at least one is recommended.'), 151 ('Another plugin already uses this name.'), 158 ('Plugin name is unique.'). User-facing reproduction confirmed: PluginSubmissionController::__invoke (line 51) calls $checker->check($plugin), persists the full $report to submission_metadata (line 54), and returns it verbatim as 'checks' in the JsonResponse (line 68) to the submitting publisher. Framework code in packages/marketplace, not vendored shadcn.

NOT DUPLICATE: The ledger has exactly ONE PluginAutoChecker entry (round 11, line 133, screenshots_count pluralization), which the source confirms is the only localized message (now using trans_choice). The 8 remaining message strings are absent from the R1-R11 ledger. The finding's claim that R11 localized only the screenshots OK message is accurate.

Confidence high: file contents, ledger, and consumer controller all directly inspected and consistent with the finding.

confirmed.title: Action failure flashes raw exception message (English) verbatim — no localized default
confirmed.detail: When a custom action throws and the app did not call ->failureNotification(...), the controller does `$message = $action->getFailureNotification() ?? $e->getMessage();` then `back()->with('error', $message)`. The raw Throwable message (English framework/PHP text, also a potential internals leak) is flashed to the user as the error notification with no localized fallback such as arqel::messages.action.failed. Framework-hardcoded gap (missing default key).
confirmed.why_real:
Verified in code at /home/diogo/PhpstormProjects/arqel/packages/actions/src/Http/Controllers/ActionController.php:135: `$message = $action->getFailureNotification() ?? $e->getMessage();` then `back()->with('error', $message)`. When an app omits ->failureNotification(), the raw Throwable message (English framework/PHP text) is flashed verbatim to the user as the error notification — user-facing, with no localized default fallback.

NOVEL / NOT A DUPLICATE: The ledger has two ActionController.php entries — line 90 ("Missing selection." hardcoded, round 8) and line 115 (action-form validation labels, round 6). Neither covers the catch-block fallback at line 135. The Action.php:327 entry (round 1) concerns serializing the app-supplied failureNotification verbatim, not the missing-default-key gap when no failureNotification is set.

GAP IS GENUINE: packages/core/resources/lang/{en,pt_BR}/messages.php define an 'action' group containing 'missing_selection' but NO 'failed' key. The proposed arqel::messages.action.failed does not exist. The sibling defect at line 90 (same file, same group, 45 lines above) was already fixed using exactly this localized pattern: __('arqel::messages.action.missing_selection') — establishing the framework's own convention that line 135 violates. Also a potential internals-leak (raw exception text surfaced to end users).

NOT VENDORED: PHP controller in packages/actions, not packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*.

Confidence high: code reproduces, ledger scanned exhaustively for ActionController and exception/fail terms, lang files confirm the missing key.

confirmed.title: ResourceController action failure flashes raw exception message (English) verbatim — same gap as ActionController
confirmed.detail: Second site of the identical pattern: in the resource-level action execution path, `$message = is_string($failureNotification) ? $failureNotification : $e->getMessage();` then `back()->with('error', $message)`. With no app-supplied failureNotification the raw English Throwable message is flashed to the end user; there is no localized default fallback. Framework-hardcoded gap.
confirmed.why_real: CONFIRMED. ResourceController.php:416 reads `$message = is_string($failureNotification) ? $failureNotification : $e->getMessage();` then `return back()->with('error', $message);` (lines 411-418). When the action declares no failureNotification, the raw English Throwable message is flashed verbatim to the end user via the flash-toast channel — a user-facing missing-i18n / no-localized-default-fallback defect. It is framework-hardcoded in packages/core (NOT a vendored packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/* primitive). The identical sibling pattern at packages/actions/src/Http/Controllers/ActionController.php:135 (`$action->getFailureNotification() ?? $e->getMessage()`) confirms the 'second site' framing is accurate. Ledger scan (reports/i18n-fixed-ledger.json): the only ResourceController.php entry is line 763 (validation :attribute snake_case rendering, round 2) — a different defect/codepath. ActionController.php entries are line 115 (validation labels, round 6) and line 90 ('Missing selection.', round 8) — neither is the catch-block getMessage() flash. The AI-controller 'raw exception leak' entries (round 9) are a different file and a JSON-API response path, not this back()->with('error') flash. No R1-R11 entry covers ResourceController.php:416. Novel, real, user-facing, un-ledgered, non-vendored.

confirmed.title: DevTools TimeTravel snapshot count uses naive English ternary pluralization
confirmed.detail: `{snapshots.length} snapshot{snapshots.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}` hardcodes English plural-by-appending-'s', which is wrong for locales whose plural rules differ (e.g. pt-BR '2 instantâneos' vs '1 instantâneo', or languages with >2 plural categories like Polish/Russian/Arabic). This is the same naive-(s) family the ledger fixed elsewhere (e.g. VersionPresenter→trans_choice, useArqelTranslations Intl.PluralRules), but this specific line was NOT in the ledger — the only TimeTravel.tsx entry covered was the toLocaleTimeString() at line 82. Repro: switch panel locale to pt-BR and observe a single snapshot still renders the English 'snapshot' with an English-only singular/plural switch; with 2 it appends a Latin 's'. Caveat/context worth flagging: the devtools-extension panel is an internal developer-facing browser extension and currently has NO translator wired (no useTranslator/useArqelTranslations/trans imports anywhere in packages-js/devtools-extension/src), so unlike the framework's end-user admin surfaces it has no i18n infrastructure at all — fixing it cleanly would require introducing a translator/Intl.PluralRules path into the extension, or it can be intentionally accepted as English-only tooling. Reported for completeness because the sweep was asked to find ALL naive '(s)'/ternary pluralization across EVERY package.
confirmed.why_real: Verified at packages-js/devtools-extension/src/panel/TimeTravel.tsx:56 — the counter renders `{snapshots.length} snapshot{snapshots.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}`, which is naive English plural-by-appending-'s' and fails for pt-BR and >2-category plural locales (Polish/Russian/Arabic). Reproduces exactly as described. NOT a duplicate: the ledger has two TimeTravel-family entries, both at round 9 — TimeTravel.tsx:82 and InertiaInspector.tsx — and both cover only the `toLocaleTimeString()` no-locale-arg pattern, not line 56's ternary pluralization. A full grep of the 249 ledger entries confirms line 56 / 'snapshot count' is never logged. Not vendored: the file is a devtools panel component, not under packages-js/ui/src/shadcn/*. Same naive-(s) family the ledger fixed elsewhere (VersionPresenter→trans_choice, useArqelTranslations Intl.PluralRules). Caveat: devtools-extension is internal developer-facing tooling with ZERO i18n infrastructure (grep confirms no useTranslator/useArqelTranslations/trans/PluralRules imports anywhere — every panel string is hardcoded English), so it is weaker than an end-user admin surface. But the project's own round-9 precedent already admitted line 82 of this exact file into the i18n ledger, so by established scope this novel sibling line is in-scope and real.
